vocab.meaning: khởi hành, bắt đầu một chuyến đi
vocab.example: I would love to set off on a journey across the Scottish Highlands at the crack of dawn.

vocab.meaning: đi về phía, hướng về một nơi nào đó
vocab.example: If I had the chance, I would head for the coastal paths of Cornwall to enjoy the sea breeze.

vocab.meaning: tận hưởng, đắm mình trong (không khí, ánh nắng)
vocab.example: Walking through Kyoto in spring would be a dream, allowing me to soak up the serene atmosphere of the temples.
I enjoy long walks in old European cities because you often stumble upon hidden gems like tiny bookstores or cafes.
I’d spend hours walking along the Seine in Paris to take in the breathtaking architecture.
The trail was so beautiful that I decided to double back just to see the waterfall from a different angle.

vocab.meaning: đi ngang qua, đi xuyên qua
vocab.example: I would love a route that passes through dense forests and opens up into vast, rolling hills.
Even if my legs were tired, the stunning views would encourage me to keep on until I reached the summit.

vocab.meaning: kết thúc ở, dừng chân tại (thường là ngoài dự tính)
vocab.example: I’d start walking without a map and see where I end up, perhaps at a secluded beach.

vocab.meaning: thoát khỏi, rời xa (sự ồn ào, áp lực)
vocab.example: A long walk in the countryside is the perfect way to get away from the frantic pace of city life.
